source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane / Other Lifting

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, Main Pass Block 296

event.top_event: Struck by wrench during stud removal operation
actions_taken: Medical evacuation to Gulf Regional Medical Center in Abbeville, Louisiana
Medical treatment administered (4 stitches to lower lip and tooth replacement)
Safety stand down held to discuss incident with all personnel
Incident reported to BSEE and submitted into eWell
Vessel cleaning operation restarted and completed without further incident
New tool (Hammer Tight Slugging Wrench Retainer) ordered for future use
event.summary: On January 21, 2019, at 06:45 hours, an Acadian Contractors Construction crew member was struck in the mouth by a backup wrench while removing studs from a manway opening on the ABJ-1400 Oil Run Surge Tank at MP 296 C. The wrench came off and struck the rigger, busting his lip and cracking his tooth. The injured person was medically evacuated and treated, receiving 4 stitches and a tooth replacement. He was released to full duty on January 22, 2019, but reassigned to Acadian's on-land shop.
key_phrases: Best Available Safety Technology not used

hazards.name: Manual stud and nut removal using hand tools
Use of backup wrench held by second person

threats.name: Backup wrench inadequately secured or held in position
Wrench slips or bounces off during hammer impact

consequences.name: Medical evacuation and lost work time

controls.name: Pre-job safety meeting and Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and plan safe work

supporting_text: At 6:00 am on January 21, 2019, platform personnel held a pre-job safety meeting and a JSA prior to beginning the task

controls.name: Two-person operation for stud removal
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control requiring second person to hold backup wrench

supporting_text: the contractor did not have the (secondary) backup hammer wrench adequately secured or held in positon as not to allow it to slip or bounce off once impacted with the hammer

controls.name: Proper tool selection and use of Best Available Safety Technology (BAST)
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to eliminate hazard through safer tool design

supporting_text: The crew did not use Best Available Safety Technology (BAST)

The failure to provide a safer method to remove the bolts prior to starting the task. There are tools designed to attach to the stud and nut that secures the backup hammer wrench, which eliminates the need for a second person holding it
GOM Shelf identified an alternative tool, a Hammer Tight Slugging Wrench Retainer, which allows personnel to tighten and loosen studs using a hammer wrench with no second person involved

controls.name: Medical evacuation and emergency response
controls.barrier_role: Mitigation control to minimize injury severity and consequences

supporting_text: GOM Shelf immediately evacuated the IP to Gulf Regional Medical Center in Abbeville, Louisiana where the medical staff administered medical treatment (4 stitches to lower lip) and a tooth replacement procedure

controls.name: Safety stand down and incident communication
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to prevent recurrence through organizational learning

supporting_text: There was a safety stand down to discuss the incident with all personnel
The Bureau of Safety and Environmental Enforcement (BSEE) received notification of the incident, and GOM Shelf later submitted the incident into eWell

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
